sound_type: deep cronk + bell + hollow knock + mimicry -- among the most complex vocalizations in the animal kingdom, over 30 distinct call types
ecosystem: Open forest. Cliffs, mountains, coastal areas, urban edges. One of the most widely distributed birds on Earth.
season: Year-round resident. Vocal throughout the year. Breeding season: late winter through spring.

coordinates.description: Reference for open forest and mountain habitat in the PNW, Washington. Ravens found throughout forested mountains, coastal cliffs, and urban edges.

taxonomy_note.description: NEW FAMILY (49th): Corvidae. AVES RETURNS after 40 degrees (last bird: Pied-billed Grebe at degree 60). Corvidae connects to Steller's Jay at degree 53 -- same family, 47 degrees apart. The raven is the largest passerine (songbird). Centennial degree.
taxonomy_note.infraclass_note: Aves returns! Last bird at degree 60. 40 degrees of mammals, amphibians, and insects between.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Corvus from Latin corvus ('raven'). corax from Greek korax ('raven'). Corvidae from Latin corvus. Common name 'raven' from Old English hraefn. The word is ancient across Indo-European languages -- the raven has been named since before recorded history.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Corvus -- crows and ravens. The largest genus in Corvidae. The Common Raven is the largest member -- the largest passerine on Earth.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Passeriformes -- songbirds. The largest order of birds (~6,500 species). The raven is the largest member of this order.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Aves -- RETURNS at degree 100! Last bird at degree 60 (Pied-billed Grebe). 40 degrees apart. After mammals (61-85), amphibians (90, 96, 97), and insects (87-89, 91, 94, 95, 98, 99), birds return at the centennial.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Corvidae is the 49th family in the engine. NEW. Same family as Steller's Jay (degree 53). The Jay introduced Corvidae; the Raven is Corvidae's full declaration.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: Variable. Deep cronk: 200-800 Hz. Bell-like tones: 1000-3000 Hz. Hollow knocks: broadband. Full repertoire spans an extraordinarily wide frequency range.
song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: Deep cronk: 300-600 Hz. The signature call has energy concentrated in low frequencies. Bell tones peak higher. Total repertoire spans multiple octaves.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Highly variable. No fixed song structure. Over 30 distinct call types used in different contexts. Vocal exchanges can be rapid and varied.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Individual cronk: 0.3-1.0 seconds. Sequences: variable. Ravens can vocalize continuously for extended periods, especially during pair interactions.
song_metrics.note_structure: Extraordinarily complex. Over 30 documented call types. No single note structure -- the raven's vocal repertoire is more like a language than a song.
song_metrics.note_types: Deep cronk (territorial, long-range). Bell-like tones (pair-bonding, close). Hollow knocks (percussive, unusual). Gargling (complex, multi-tonal). Screams (alarm). Soft warbles (intimate). Mimicry (learned, cultural). And many more.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Variable. Context-dependent. Ravens combine call types in sequences that vary by social context, emotional state, and individual. Some phrases are culturally transmitted.
song_metrics.timbre: Deep, resonant, hollow for the cronk. Clear, ringing, metallic for bell tones. Wooden, percussive for knocks. Raspy for gargling. Highly variable. The most timbral diversity of any species in the engine.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. The deep cronk is loud and carrying -- audible at several hundred meters. Bell tones and knocks are moderate range. Soft warbles are intimate. The overall vocal presence is strong.
song_metrics.tempo: Variable. Ravens adjust calling rate by context. Rapid exchanges during pair interactions. Slow, deliberate cronks during territorial display.
song_metrics.context: MULTI-FUNCTIONAL. Territorial defense (cronk). Pair-bonding (bell, warble). Alarm (scream). Play (variable). Food discovery (specific calls). Social coordination (complex sequences). The vocal repertoire serves every aspect of social life.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Significant. Raven vocalizations show geographic variation and cultural transmission. Local dialects documented. Individual recognition by voice. Cultural learning of novel sounds.
song_metrics.key_property: DEEP CRONK + BELL + HOLLOW KNOCK + MIMICRY -- the 100th acoustic element (species side). Among the most complex vocalizations in the animal kingdom. Over 30 distinct call types. E=4. CORVIDAE -- NEW FAMILY (49th). AVES RETURNS after 40 degrees. Open forest habitat. Fortieth consecutive original CSV. CENTENNIAL DEGREE. One of the most intelligent animals on Earth.

plumage.overall: Entirely black. Iridescent purple-blue-green sheen on feathers when viewed in sunlight. Large, powerful, commanding presence.
plumage.diagnostic_mark: For degree 100: (1) ENTIRELY BLACK -- no white markings. (2) LARGE SIZE -- largest passerine, wingspan ~120-150 cm. (3) WEDGE-SHAPED TAIL -- diagnostic in flight (crows have fan-shaped). (4) THICK, HEAVY BILL -- much larger than crow. (5) SHAGGY THROAT FEATHERS -- hackles. (6) DEEP CRONK CALL -- diagnostic (crows caw, ravens cronk).
plumage.flight_pattern: Soaring flight on flat wings (crows flap constantly). Aerial acrobatics during play -- barrel rolls, inversions, dives. Powerful, confident flight.

physical_description.overall: Largest passerine on Earth. Body length 54-67 cm. Wingspan 115-150 cm. Weight 0.7-1.5 kg. Entirely black with iridescent sheen. Heavy bill. Shaggy throat hackles. Wedge-shaped tail.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: For degree 100: (1) SIZE -- much larger than crows. (2) WEDGE TAIL -- in flight, diagnostic. (3) THICK BILL -- heavy, curved. (4) HACKLES -- shaggy throat feathers. (5) DEEP VOICE -- cronk, not caw. (6) SOARING FLIGHT -- flat wings.
physical_description.field_id: Very large black bird, larger than a crow. Wedge-shaped tail in flight (crow = fan). Heavy, curved bill. Shaggy throat hackles. Soars on flat wings (crow flaps). Deep cronk call (crow caws). If the black bird soars, cronks, and seems impossibly large -- it is a raven.

nodes.title: Deep Cronk + Bell + Hollow Knock -- 100th Acoustic Element (species side)
nodes.description: Among the most complex vocalizations in the animal kingdom. Over 30 distinct call types. Deep cronk, bell-like tones, hollow knocks, gargling, screams, warbles, mimicry. E=4. The most diverse vocal repertoire in the engine.

nodes.title: Corvidae -- NEW FAMILY (49th)
nodes.description: Corvids enter the engine. Same family as Steller's Jay (53). The raven is the corvid apex -- largest passerine, most vocal, most intelligent. 47 degrees between corvid entries.

nodes.title: AVES RETURNS -- After 40 Degrees, Birds Are Back
nodes.description: Last bird at degree 60 (Pied-billed Grebe). 40 degrees of mammals, amphibians, and insects. Aves returns at degree 100 -- the centennial. Birds are back.

nodes.title: Intelligence -- Tool Use, Problem-Solving, Play, Cultural Transmission
nodes.description: One of the most intelligent animals on Earth. Tool use and manufacture. Multi-step problem-solving. Future planning. Causal reasoning. Play behavior. Social cognition. Theory of mind. Cultural transmission of learned behaviors.

nodes.title: The Centennial -- 100th Degree, 27.8% of 360
nodes.description: 100th degree. 27.8% of 360. Corvidae enters and Aves returns at the centennial. The raven marks the milestone. Fortieth consecutive original CSV -- FOUR DECADES.

nodes.title: The Summer Heart Contrast -- Electronic and Biological Complexity
nodes.description: Both E=4. Both complex, layered voices. Summer Heart's nostalgic cascade layers synth textures. The raven layers 30+ call types. Electronic cascading and biological vocal orchestra.

cross_references.relationship: SAME FAMILY CORVIDAE! First corvid at degree 53. Same family, 47 degrees apart. The Jay introduced Corvidae; the Raven is Corvidae's full declaration. Both intelligent, both vocal. Jay is bright and loud; Raven is deep and complex.
INTELLIGENCE AND OLD FOREST. Both highly intelligent birds of old forest habitats. Owl hunts silently; Raven vocalizes boldly. Both apex predators. Nocturnal stealth vs diurnal cognition.

INTELLIGENCE PARALLEL. Both among the most intelligent in the engine. Fox = Canidae intelligence (adaptable, cunning). Raven = Corvidae intelligence (tool use, cultural transmission). Different phyla, convergent cognition.

INTELLIGENCE AND DEXTERITY. Both highly intelligent, both problem-solvers. Raccoon manipulates with hands; Raven manipulates with beak and cognition. Both urban-adaptable. Mammalian vs avian intelligence.
AVES RETURNS. Both large, commanding birds with unforgettable voices. Heron's pterodactyl croak at degree 14. Raven's complex repertoire at degree 100. The raven marks Aves' return after 40 degrees.
